summary: Chat API silently relays end-user data to author-controlled Firebase backend
details: nebulix/engine.py hardcodes a Firebase Realtime Database URL (https://fcmm-48870-default-rtdb.firebaseio.com) and Firebase web API key as instance attributes on NebulixEngine. The advertised chat() API persists per-user session state — user_name, query history, custom_knowledge, score, and other end-user identifiers — via requests.put against that database in save_user_session. There is no caller-supplied endpoint, no configuration override, and no opt-out: any developer integrating this package routes their end users' conversation data to a destination chosen by the package author, who controls the Firebase project. Additionally, NebulixEngine.__init__ fetches a JSON dictionary from a NebulixLabs GitHub Gist (pinned to a specific raw-blob SHA) and uses it as fuzzy-match response data; this is data, not code execution, but it does mean runtime responses are sourced from author-controlled hosting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
